serverComponents.title: Server Components
serverComponents.description: In Next.js, all components are Server Components by default. They run on the server, can access databases directly, and send zero JavaScript to the client.
serverComponents.sectionThisPage: This Page IS a Server Component
serverComponents.demoDescription: These posts were fetched directly from the database in this Server Component. No API route, no useEffect, no loading state — just a direct query.
serverComponents.totalPosts: Total: {count} posts fetched at render time. Check the network tab — no JS bundle for this data!
serverComponents.keyZeroClientJsDesc: Server Components send only HTML to the client. No JavaScript bundle for rendering — the browser just displays the result.
serverComponents.keyAsyncComponentsDesc: Server Components can be async functions. Use await directly at the component level — no useEffect or loading state needed.
serverComponents.keyDirectBackendDesc: Access databases, file systems, and environment secrets directly. No API layer needed for data that only the server sees.
serverComponents.keyDefaultDesc: Every component is a Server Component unless you add "use client". Start with server, add "use client" only when you need interactivity.

clientComponents.title: Client Components
clientComponents.description: Add 'use client' to opt into interactivity. Client Components can use hooks, event handlers, and browser APIs.
clientComponents.sectionInteractiveDemosDesc: These components use 'use client' — they run in the browser and can respond to user interaction.
clientComponents.boundaryEffectDesc: When you mark a file as "use client", everything it imports also becomes part of the client bundle.
clientComponents.keyOptInDesc: Components are Server Components by default. Only add "use client" when you need interactivity. Keep the boundary as deep as possible.
clientComponents.keyBundleImpactDesc: Everything imported by a "use client" file becomes part of the client JS bundle. This is why you should push the boundary down to leaf components.
clientComponents.keyPropsSerializableDesc: When a Server Component passes props to a Client Component, the props must be serializable (no functions, no classes, no Date objects).
clientComponents.keyHooksNeedClientDesc: useState, useEffect, useRef, and all React hooks only work in Client Components. The "use client" directive is your entry point to React's interactive features.
clientComponents.counterDescription: useState + onClick — only possible in Client Components.

composition.title: Composition Pattern
composition.description: The most powerful pattern in Next.js: Server Components pass children to Client Components. Interactive wrappers, server-rendered content.
composition.sectionLiveDemo: Live Demo — Server Content in Client Wrapper
composition.sectionLiveDemoDesc: The collapsible card below is a Client Component (interactive). But its CONTENT was rendered on the server.
composition.serverDataNote: This data was fetched on the server (not in the client bundle)
composition.serverTimestampNote: This timestamp was generated on the server at render time
composition.serializationTitle: What Can Be Passed as Props?
composition.serArraysObjects: Arrays and plain objects (of serializable values)
composition.serNoFunctions: Functions, classes, Date objects, Map, Set
composition.serNoEventHandlers: Event handlers (pass from client, not server)

state.title: State Management (Zustand)
state.description: Zustand provides lightweight, hook-based state management that works perfectly with Next.js Client Components. No providers needed.
state.sectionLiveDemoDesc: Two separate Client Components sharing the same Zustand store. Add items on the left, see them appear on the right.
state.keyNoProviderDesc: Unlike Redux or Context, Zustand stores work without wrapping your app in a Provider. Just import and use the hook anywhere.
state.keyClientOnlyDesc: Zustand uses React hooks, so it only works in Client Components. For server data, use Server Components with direct DB access instead.
state.keySelectiveRerendersDesc: Use selectors to subscribe to specific state slices. Components only re-render when their selected state changes — great for performance.
state.keyTinyBundleDesc: Zustand is ~1KB gzipped. No boilerplate, no reducers, no action creators. Define state and actions in one place.
state.cartEmpty: Cart is empty. Add some products!
